chunks.title: JavaScript Local Storage & Session Storage
chunks.text: Web Storage API allows you to store data in the browser.

chunks.title: JavaScript Local Storage & Session Storage
chunks.text: There are two types: localStorage and sessionStorage.

chunks.title: JavaScript Local Storage & Session Storage
chunks.text: localStorage data persists even after the browser is closed.

chunks.title: JavaScript Local Storage & Session Storage
chunks.text: sessionStorage data is cleared when the browser tab is closed.

chunks.title: JavaScript Local Storage & Session Storage
chunks.text: Both store data as key-value pairs where both key and value are strings.

chunks.title: JavaScript Local Storage & Session Storage
chunks.text: localStorage.setItem sets a key-value pair in local storage.

chunks.title: JavaScript Local Storage & Session Storage
chunks.text: localStorage.getItem retrieves the value for a given key.

chunks.title: JavaScript Local Storage & Session Storage
chunks.text: localStorage.removeItem removes a specific key-value pair.

chunks.title: JavaScript Local Storage & Session Storage
chunks.text: localStorage.clear removes all data from local storage.

chunks.title: JavaScript Local Storage & Session Storage
chunks.text: Since localStorage only stores strings, you need to use JSON methods for objects and arrays.

chunks.title: JavaScript Local Storage & Session Storage
chunks.text: Use JSON.stringify to convert an object to a string before storing.

chunks.title: JavaScript Local Storage & Session Storage
chunks.text: Use JSON.parse to convert the string back to an object when retrieving.

chunks.title: JavaScript Local Storage & Session Storage
chunks.text: The storage limit is usually around 5 to 10 megabytes depending on the browser.

chunks.title: JavaScript Local Storage & Session Storage
chunks.text: localStorage is great for saving user preferences, theme settings, and simple data.

chunks.title: JavaScript Local Storage & Session Storage
chunks.text: Never store sensitive data like passwords or tokens in localStorage.

chunks.title: JavaScript Local Storage & Session Storage
chunks.text: The storage event fires when localStorage is modified in another tab.

chunks.title: JavaScript Local Storage & Session Storage
chunks.text: This can be used to sync data across multiple tabs of the same website.

chunks.title: JavaScript Local Storage & Session Storage
chunks.text: Local storage is a simple but powerful tool for client-side data persistence.

chunks.title: JavaScript Local Storage & Session Storage
chunks.text: Many real-world applications use localStorage for caching and offline functionality.

text: Web Storage API allows you to store data in the browser. There are two types: localStorage and sessionStorage. localStorage data persists even after the browser is closed. sessionStorage data is cleared when the browser tab is closed. Both store data as key-value pairs where both key and value are strings. localStorage.setItem sets a key-value pair in local storage. localStorage.getItem retrieves the value for a given key. localStorage.removeItem removes a specific key-value pair. localStorage.clear removes all data from local storage. Since localStorage only stores strings, you need to use JSON methods for objects and arrays. Use JSON.stringify to convert an object to a string before storing. Use JSON.parse to convert the string back to an object when retrieving. The storage limit is usually around 5 to 10 megabytes depending on the browser. localStorage is great for saving user preferences, theme settings, and simple data. Never store sensitive data like passwords or tokens in localStorage. The storage event fires when localStorage is modified in another tab. This can be used to sync data across multiple tabs of the same website. Local storage is a simple but powerful tool for client-side data persistence. Many real-world applications use localStorage for caching and offline functionality.
